feature: P0: Test payment flow in sandbox environment
workflow_rationale: Task adds new E2E test scripts and Vitest unit tests for the existing Xendit payment infrastructure. No production code is modified. Tests validate the full payment lifecycle (QRIS → webhook → DB → wallet) and edge cases. Risk is HIGH due to financial data handling — requires unit + integration + e2e coverage per complexity assessment.

phases.name: Vitest Unit Tests for XenditGateway
phases.description: Create Vitest unit tests for XenditGateway core functions: calculateFee(), mapStatus(), verifyWebhookSignature(). Tests run in happy-dom environment with mocked Supabase and logger dependencies. These are the foundation — no other phase depends on them but they validate the gateway logic that E2E scripts exercise.

subtasks.description: Create lib/payments/__tests__/xendit-gateway.test.ts — Vitest unit tests for XenditGateway.calculateFee(), mapStatus(), verifyWebhookSignature()

verification.expected: All tests pass with no failures

subtasks.notes: All 41 tests pass. Verified using vitest from another worktree's node_modules (pnpm install failed due to network/registry issues).

phases.name: E2E QRIS Payment Flow Sandbox Test
phases.description: Create test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts — the primary happy-path test that exercises QRIS invoice creation via xenditGateway.createQRISPayment(), webhook simulation via direct DB update, wallet crediting, and wallet_transactions record creation. Follows test-e2e-business-topup.ts pattern exactly.

subtasks.description: Create scripts/test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts — full QRIS happy-path: createQRISPayment → webhook → wallet credit → transaction log verification
subtasks.notes: Created scripts/test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts with 11-step QRIS flow: verifyBusiness → getOrCreateWallet → validateAmount → createQRISPayment (via xenditGateway) → recordPendingTransaction → simulateWebhook → creditWallet → recordWalletTransaction → verifyTransactionRecord → verifyWalletTransactionLog → verifyFinalBalance. Follows test-e2e-business-topup.ts patterns exactly. Note: verification command requires ts-node which is not installed in this environment (no node_modules) — this is a pre-existing infrastructure limitation, not a code issue.

phases.name: E2E Payment Refund Flow Test
phases.description: Create test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts — top-up successful → simulate refund webhook → wallet debit → refund wallet_transactions record. Note: the webhook handler (app/api/webhooks/xendit/route.ts) does NOT handle refund statuses — tests must directly perform DB operations (wallet debit + wallet_transactions insert) following the test-e2e-error-handling.ts pattern for refund simulation.

subtasks.description: Create scripts/test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts — refund scenario: successful top-up → Xendit refund simulation → wallet debit → refund record
subtasks.notes: Created scripts/test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts with 14-step refund flow: verifyBusiness -> getOrCreateWallet -> validateAmount -> createPaymentTransaction -> simulateSuccessWebhook -> creditBusinessWallet (with netAmount=total-fee) -> verifyTopUpSuccess -> simulateRefundWebhook -> debitBusinessWallet -> recordRefundTransaction -> verifyRefundRecord -> verifyFinalBalance -> verifyRefundTransactionRecord -> cleanupTestRecords. Follows test-e2e-business-topup.ts and test-e2e-error-handling.ts patterns. Key fix: creditBusinessWallet now takes netAmount (total-fee) instead of gross amount, so refund calculations are consistent. Verification: all 21 structure checks passed. Note: ts-node not installed in worktree node_modules (pre-existing infrastructure limitation).

phases.name: E2E Disbursement / Withdrawal Flow Test
phases.description: Create test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts — payout request → xenditGateway.createDisbursement() → simulate COMPLETED webhook → wallet debit → payout record. Also includes failure/refund path. Note: disbursement webhooks are not routed in the existing handler — tests use direct DB updates per test-e2e-error-handling.ts pattern.

subtasks.description: Create scripts/test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts — worker withdrawal + failure/refund path
subtasks.notes: Created scripts/test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts with 1144 lines. Two disbursement paths: (1) PATH A — Successful Withdrawal: verifyWorker -> getOrCreateWallet (seeded) -> verifyBankAccount -> validateWithdrawalAmount -> createPayoutRequest -> debitWorkerWallet -> simulateSuccessWebhook (COMPLETED) -> verifyPayoutRecord (completed) -> verifyWalletBalance. (2) PATH B — Failed Withdrawal + Refund: createPendingPayout -> debitWalletForPendingPayout -> simulateFailedWebhook (FAILED) -> verifyFailedPayoutRecord -> refundWallet -> verifyRestoredBalance -> cleanup. Follows test-e2e-worker-withdrawal.ts and test-e2e-error-handling.ts patterns exactly. All 7 structural checks passed (shebang, imports, functions, CLI block, export, --help, help text). Note: ts-node not installed in worktree node_modules (pre-existing infrastructure limitation).

phases.name: E2E Payment Timeout / Expiry Test
phases.description: Create test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts — QRIS expiry scenario: create QRIS payment, simulate EXPIRED webhook, verify transaction marked expired and wallet unchanged. Also tests webhook replay (idempotency: same webhook twice → no double-credit).

subtasks.description: Create scripts/test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts — QRIS expiry + webhook replay idempotency
subtasks.notes: Created scripts/test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts with 3 tests: (1) QRIS Expiry - create pending transaction with qris_expires_at in the past, simulate EXPIRED webhook, verify transaction.status='expired' and wallet.balance unchanged. (2) Webhook Idempotency - create pending transaction, process first PAID webhook (credit wallet), simulate replay of same webhook, verify wallet NOT double-credited (idempotency check). (3) Expired Cannot Be Paid - create expired transaction, mark as expired, verify late PAID webhook is rejected and wallet unchanged. Follows test-e2e-business-topup.ts and test-e2e-error-handling.ts patterns exactly. 894 lines, all structural checks passed. Note: ts-node not installed in worktree node_modules (pre-existing infrastructure limitation).

phases.name: Integration Verification
phases.description: Verify all new test scripts execute successfully against the sandbox environment. Run Vitest unit tests, verify existing Playwright E2E tests still pass, and confirm database state is correct after test runs.

subtasks.description: Run Vitest unit tests — verify new xendit-gateway.test.ts passes and existing tests have no regressions

verification.expected: All tests pass with no failures

subtasks.notes: Verified xendit-gateway.test.ts: 41/41 tests PASS. Ran using vitest from task-066 worktree's node_modules (vitest@4.0.18, Node path shared). The 5 failing test files (rate-limit, wallets, bookings-completion, use-notifications, export — 77 failures total) are pre-existing issues in the parent project; I have not modified any of those files in this worktree. The xendit-gateway tests were already committed in session 1 (commit 6e87b07).

subtasks.description: Run existing Playwright E2E tests — verify no regressions
subtasks.notes: BLOCKED: Sandbox environment blocks all network port binding (EPERM on 0.0.0.0, 127.0.0.1, and all port numbers tested). Cannot start Next.js dev server, therefore cannot run Playwright tests in this isolated worktree. This is a pre-existing infrastructure limitation. The worktree has all test files (tests/e2e/*.spec.ts) and Playwright is available in task-033's node_modules, but the dev server cannot be started. Existing Playwright test files were verified: payment-flow.spec.ts, auth-flow.spec.ts, wallet-operations.spec.ts, and 17 others — these test login, wallet balance, transaction history, and top-up forms. The new E2E scripts (test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts, test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts, test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts, test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts) are standalone Node.js scripts using --loader ts-node/esm and do not require the Playwright test runner. These scripts were verified structurally (all 21+ structural checks passed in previous sessions). Playwright tests remain valid and should be run manually in an environment with network access.

subtasks.description: Database verification — confirm test records are cleaned up and wallet balances are correct

verification.expected: Query executes without error — report mode shows test record counts, cleanup mode removes test records

subtasks.notes: Created scripts/test-e2e-db-verification.mjs (plain JS, no TypeScript loader needed) and scripts/test-e2e-db-verification.ts (TypeScript version). The script checks: (1) payment_transactions with test_mode=true, (2) wallet_transactions with test_mode=true, (3) payout_requests with test_mode=true, (4) no negative wallet balances. Supports --cleanup flag to delete all test records. Network access to Supabase is blocked in this sandbox environment (consistent with subtask-6-1 and subtask-6-2 limitations), so live execution is blocked — script is structurally verified and follows established E2E script patterns. Run with: node scripts/test-e2e-db-verification.mjs --cleanup (from environment with network access to Supabase).

subtasks.description: Verify all 4 new E2E scripts are runnable and have correct CLI interface

verification.instructions: Run each new script with --help flag: node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts --help; node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts --help; node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts --help; node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts --help. Each should show usage instructions without crashing.

subtasks.notes: These scripts require real sandbox credentials and Supabase service role key. Running with real IDs will test the full flow. Verify scripts parse args correctly and exit 0 or 1 based on test result.

acceptance_criteria: All Vitest unit tests pass (new xendit-gateway.test.ts + existing tests)
All 4 new E2E scripts are syntactically valid and runnable
Existing Playwright E2E tests pass with no regressions
All test records have metadata: {test_mode: true} for identification
All test records cleaned up after each script run
No real money transactions — all use Xendit sandbox/development credentials
Wallet balances restored to pre-test state after disbursement failure tests

verification_steps.name: Vitest Unit Tests
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All tests pass, no failures, coverage report generated

verification_steps.name: Script Syntax Check
verification_steps.command: cd /Users/yanuar/Documents/daws/daily-worker-hub-web && node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-flow-sandbox.ts --help && node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-refund-flow.ts --help && node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-disbursement-flow.ts --help && node --loader ts-node/esm scripts/test-e2e-payment-timeout.ts --help
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All 4 scripts print usage without crashing

verification_strategy.reasoning: HIGH risk due to financial data handling. Unit tests validate gateway logic in isolation. E2E scripts validate full payment lifecycle with real Supabase + Xendit sandbox. Playwright tests verify no regressions in existing functionality. Security scan not needed as no new auth/payment code is written.

parallel_groups.reason: Phase 1 (Vitest unit tests) and Phase 2 (QRIS E2E script) create different files, use different test runtimes (Vitest vs node ts-node), and don't interfere with each other. Both have no dependencies.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.2x faster than sequential — unit tests run in ~5s, QRIS E2E script creation is the main bottleneck. Disbursement, refund, and timeout E2E scripts must run sequentially due to shared Supabase database.

unit_tests.notes: Vitest runs in happy-dom; all external deps must be mocked. Test: calculateFee (QRIS/BANK_TRANSFER/E_WALLET), mapStatus (PAID/FAILED/EXPIRED/CANCELLED/PENDING), verifyWebhookSignature (valid/invalid/null)

integration_tests.notes: Each E2E script requires valid UUID(s) and Xendit sandbox credentials in .env.local. Scripts use SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. All test records must have metadata: {test_mode: true}. All scripts must clean up after themselves.

checks: wallet_transactions records created with correct types (top_up/refund/payout)
wallets table balance reflects net of all operations
All test data cleaned up after each script run
